prompt:
Our vault staging script is quietly wrecking the nightly backup and I only caught it because I went looking for a file after the fact. The script is `vaultcopy.ps1` — it reads a manifest of relative paths (relative to the ops checkout root, where the script lives) and stages each file into the folder the vault agent sweeps. Three separate things are going on.

First: run by hand from the checkout root, it insists some files don't exist that absolutely do. `reports/q3 review [draft 2].md` is RIGHT THERE on disk — I can open it — and every night the report says `missing reports/q3 review [draft 2].md`. It's always the ones with the ugly names from the ops share.

Second, and this is the scary one: `logs/job[2].log` gets reported as copied, and a file with that name shows up in the staging folder — but the bytes are wrong. I diffed the staged copy against the real `logs/job[2].log` and they don't match; the staged one is byte-identical to `logs/job2.log`, the rotated log sitting next to it. So the backup 'succeeds' and archives the wrong file. Nobody would ever notice until a restore.

Third: the cron wrapper (which cd's to / before running anything) gets NOTHING staged — its report says missing for every single entry, even the boring ones like `configs/site.yaml`, and the same invocation works when I run it from the checkout.

Fix vaultcopy.ps1 and prove it: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_vaultcopy.ps1` from the workspace root must pass. Don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P8 item 4: multi-bug hunt, 3 related causes, all three of the catalog's exact bugs, awkward-real-world fixture names ('q3 review [draft 2].md', 'job[2].log' with rotated twin 'job2.log', 'decom checklist.txt'). Bug 1: `Test-Path $src` treats [..] as a wildcard char class — 'q3 review [draft 2].md' matches nothing literal, so an existing file reports missing. Bug 2: `Copy-Item $src` wildcard-matches 'job[2].log' onto the twin 'job2.log' and stages the WRONG BYTES under the right name (probed on this box 2026-07-16; also probed: unmatched wildcard source makes Copy-Item a silent no-op, which is how a leave-one-out variant fails the content check). Bug 3: entries resolve against `(Get-Location).Path` instead of $PSScriptRoot, so the cron wrapper running from / stages nothing; harness drives the tool from two different cwds via Push-Location (child pwsh inherits cwd — probed). Fixes: -LiteralPath on both cmdlets + $PSScriptRoot. Leave-one-out proven at authoring time: each variant with exactly one bug left fails (bug1 -> report mismatch on the lonely bracket name; bug2 -> staged-bytes mismatch AND silent-no-op absence; bug3 -> elsewhere-run all missing); verify run 5x identical. Prompt is symptom-first (wrong 'missing', wrong bytes, cwd-dependent) and never names Test-Path, Copy-Item, -LiteralPath, or $PSScriptRoot. Differentiation vs bash-backupsel/zsh-backupsel and ps-oddwalk (6P4 fs build): this is a debug seed about PowerShell's wildcard-by-default provider paths, a failure mode bash globs don't have (bash [..] happens in the shell, not inside test -e).
